game_name: Dungeons & Dragons: Eye of the Beholder

element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Core mechanic taught through initial dungeon sections
Combat rules gradually applied in escalating encounter difficulty
Awkward interface requires learning curve; more complex than weapon combat
Skeletal resurrection and NPC discovery; series tradition of party expansion
Emphasis on puzzles over pure combat; increases as player descends
Territorial encounters while navigating clan territories
More threatening faction before final descent
Ultimate threat; final confrontation after sewer entrance collapse

typical_relief_triggers: Safe rest areas between faction territories
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Methodical descent with escalating difficulty; puzzle-solving breaks combat tension; abrupt ending noted as jarring by critics

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: NPC locations scattered throughout; skeletal resurrection corpses discoverable

difficulty_curve.early_game: Accessible with initial 4-character party; basic combat and spell introduction
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Escalating encounter difficulty; Dwarf and Drow clan navigation; increased puzzle complexity
difficulty_curve.late_game: Intense pressure approaching Xanathar's lair; party coordination required; limited resources

notable_level_patterns.description: Player descends from surface sewers through Dwarf territory, then Drow territory, finally reaching Xanathar's lair. Each faction occupies dedicated spatial zones with territorial encounters.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Dungeon descent structure (sewers → dwarf clans → drow clans → beholder lair)

notable_level_patterns.description: Sewer entrance collapse by Xanathar prevents retreat; forces players to commit to deeper descent and seek alternative paths through clan territories.
Emphasis on environmental puzzles and problem-solving rather than relying solely on combat strength; praised as refreshing change from previous AD&D titles.

sources_used: wikipedia: Eye of the Beholder (video game)
